definition.name: NXpeak

symbols.doc: The symbols used in the schema to specify e.g. dimensions of arrays.

definition.doc: Base class for spectral peaks, their functional form, and support (values).

field.name: label
field.doc: Human-readable label which specifies which concept/entity the peak represents/identifies.

field.name: description
field.doc:
Is the peak described analytically via a functional form or is it empirically defined via measured/reported intensity/counts as a function of an independent variable.
If the functional form is not empirical or Gaussians, users should enter other for the peak_model and add relevant details in the NXcollection.

field.name: peak_index
field.doc: Index of the peak, for determining the numbering of multiple NXpeak instances. Starts with 1.

field.name: position
field.doc: In the case of an empirical description of the peak and its shoulders, this array holds the position values for the independent variable.

field.name: intensity
field.doc: In the case of an empirical description of the peak and its shoulders, this array holds the intensity/count values at each position.

field.name: width
field.doc:
Width of a peak at a defined fraction of the peak height.
Usually, this will be the Full Width at Half Maximum of the peak (FWHM). For asymmetrical peaks, convenient measures of peak width are the half-widths of each side of the peak at half maximum intensity.

item.doc: Full Width at Half Maximum of the peak.
Half Width at Half Maximum of the peak.
For asymmetrical peaks, the half-widths of each side of the peak at half maximum intensity is given.

field.name: line_shape
field.doc: Human-readable label of the line shapes used for the peak.

field.name: functional_form
field.doc: A formula to describe the line shape using set of parameters as entered by the `peak_parameters` fields.

group.name: peak_parameters
group.doc: In the case of an analytical description (or if description is other) this collection holds parameter of (and eventually) the functional form. For example in the case of Gaussians mu, sigma, cut-off values, and background intensity are relevant parameter.

field.name: total_area
field.doc: Total area under the curve (can also be used for the total area minus any background values.)

field.name: relative_area
field.doc:
Relative area of this peak compared to other peaks in an NXpeak_model.
The relative area can simply be derived by dividing the total_area by the total area of all peaks or by a more complicated method (e.g., by additionally dividing by the relative_sensitivity_factor). Details should be stored in the quantification description of NXpeak_model.

field.name: relative_sensitivity_factor
field.doc:
Relative sensitivity for this peak, to be used in quantification in an NXpeak_model.
As an example, in X-ray spectroscopy could depend on the energy scale (see position), the ionization cross section, and the element probed.

group.name: area_constraint
group.doc: Constraint on the minimum and maximum area of this peak.

field.name: depends_on
field.doc: Link to a peak the area constraint depends on.

group.name: width_constraint
group.doc: Constraint on the minimum and maximum width of this peak.

field.name: depends_on
field.doc: Link to a peak the width constraint depends on.

group.name: position_constraint
group.doc: Constraint on the minimum and maximum position of this peak on the abscissa.

field.name: depends_on
field.doc: Link to a peak the position constraint depends on.
